title: Tabernaemontana gamblei
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana gamblei belongs to the family Apocynaceae and the order Gentianales. It is a species of flowering plant native to India. The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Tabernaemontana and the subtribe Tabernaemontaneae. This species is also known as the 'Gamble's tabernaemontana'. It is closely related to other species of the same genus.
uses: Tabernaemontana gamblei is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and fight infections. Its extracts are also used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, Tabernaemontana gamblei is reported to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.

In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant may lead to more severe side effects, including cardiac problems and neurological symptoms.
